articles.title: U.S. Said to Have Sent Iran a 15-Point Plan to End the Middle East War
articles.snippet: The 15-point plan was delivered via Pakistan, whose army chief has emerged as the key interlocutor between the United States and Iran, officials say.

articles.title: U.S. Drafts Plan to End War With Iran as 2,000 Paratroopers Ordered to Deploy
articles.snippet: Nearly a month into the war with Iran, the Trump administration is keeping its options open: it has drafted a 15-point plan to end the war and is ordering thousands of paratroopers to deploy in the Middle East.

articles.title: Iran Says 'Non-Hostile' Ships Can Sail Through the Strait of Hormuz
articles.snippet: Ships with no ties to Israel or the United States would be allowed to pass, the government said, but it was unclear if any vessels would try.

articles.title: Audio From Cockpit Recorder Reveals Error Before Deadly LaGuardia Crash
articles.snippet: LaGuardia's air traffic control tower failed to recognize it had granted permission for a plane and an emergency vehicle to use the same runway, officials said.

articles.title: Trump Says Iran Gave the US a 'Very Big Present' but Gives No Detail
articles.snippet: Donald Trump gave the update on Tuesday as the US-Israeli war with Iran entered its fourth week.

articles.title: Democrat Emily Gregory Wins Florida Special Election in Mar-a-Lago's District
articles.snippet: Emily Gregory's victory in Palm Beach brought the Democratic surge to President Trump's backyard, while a union leader leads in a race for a state senate seat vacated by Florida's lieutenant governor.

articles.title: OpenAI Pulls the Plug on Sora, the Viral AI Video App That Sparked Deepfake Concerns
articles.snippet: OpenAI said Tuesday that it was "saying goodbye to the Sora app" and that it would share more soon about how to preserve what users already created on the app.

articles.title: Boss of Financial Giant BlackRock Warns of Global Recession if Oil Price Hits $150
articles.snippet: Larry Fink says if oil prices stay high for a sustained period it will have "profound implications" for the world economy.

articles.title: Senate Republicans Again Block Bid to Halt Iran War Without Authorization
articles.snippet: Senate Democrats failed for the third time to advance a resolution that would force the removal of troops from hostilities in Iran unless Congress approves offensive operations.

articles.title: Your Data Is Everywhere. The Government Is Buying It Without a Warrant
articles.snippet: Data brokers buy up huge amounts of information from cell phones and browsers to sell for targeted advertising. But the government, including ICE, also buys the data.
